description_long: This object is a medium-sized airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a distinct cockpit area at the front. The wings are mounted low on the fuselage and extend outward with a straight configuration, lacking visible winglets. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The exterior is primarily white with some red and gray accents, and the surface appears hard and smooth, typical of metal-skinned aircraft. The landing gear is visible and appears to be a tricycle configuration with two main gear units and a nose wheel. The airplane is large enough to be enterable and likely supports multiple passengers or crew inside.
description: A medium-sized, white airplane with low-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and a forward cockpit. The exterior is smooth and hard, with visible tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, cockpit at the front, and the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, and the forward placement of the landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wingspan and the tail section, with less detail on the cockpit.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the back of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines; engine type and placement not clearly shown.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
